rulings.Case Title: ELIZABETH DIANE SCHENK VS. ELIZABETH JANE MCCARTHY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce Settlement Against Defendant Charlie Barnett
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 30, 2025 line 1. PLAINTIFF ELIZABETH SCHENK INDIV AND AS TRUSTEE OF THE POWER OF APPOINTMENT TRUST, UNDER THE 2004 SCHENK FAMILY TRUST DATED 2/9/2004 NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO ENFORCE SETTLEMENT AGAINST DEFENDANT CHARLIE BARNETT is OFF CALENDAR, per Plaintiff's May 19, 2025 filing. =(501/VMH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: ALI A. AMINI ET AL VS. ANDREW LINTON ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 30, 2025 line 7. DEFENDANT ANGELINA GREEP MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS is DENIED. Defendant Greep shall serve and file a response within 5 days of service of notice of entry of order. Service under CCP 415.45 was properly completed by posting at the premises and mailing by certified mail. No further action was required to complete service. Confirmation of delivery of the mailed copy is not required under the statute, and the subsequent interference by a co-defendant with the posted copy, after it was properly posted, does not invalidate the completed service. =(501/VMH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
